title: HetznerLoadBalancerService
description: Manages a service (listener) on a Hetzner Cloud Load Balancer.

LoadBalancerServiceProtocol.description: Protocol of the Load Balancer service.

domain.description: Host header to send in the HTTP request. May not contain spaces, percent or backslash symbols. Can be null, in that case no host header is sent.

path.description: HTTP path to use for health checks. May not contain literal spaces, use percent-encoding instead.

response.description: String that must be contained in HTTP response in order to pass the health check.

statusCodes.description: List of returned HTTP status codes in order to pass the health check. Supports the wildcards `?` for exactly one character and `*` for multiple ones.

port.description: Port the health check will be performed on.

interval.description: Time interval in seconds health checks are performed.

timeout.description: Time in seconds after an attempt is considered a timeout.

retries.description: Unsuccessful retries needed until a target is considered unhealthy; an unhealthy target needs the same number of successful retries to become healthy again.

LoadBalancerServiceHttp.description: Configuration option for protocols http and https.

cookieName.description: Name of the cookie used for sticky sessions.

cookieLifetime.description: Lifetime of the cookie used for sticky sessions (in seconds).

certificates.description: IDs of the Certificates to use for TLS/SSL termination by the Load Balancer; empty for TLS/SSL passthrough or if `protocol` is `http`.

redirectHttp.description: Redirect HTTP requests to HTTPS. Only available if `protocol` is `https`.

stickySessions.description: Use sticky sessions. Only available if `protocol` is `http` or `https`.

loadBalancerId.description: ID of the Load Balancer this service belongs to.

listenPort.description: Port the Load Balancer listens on.

protocol.description: Protocol of the Load Balancer service.

destinationPort.description: Port the Load Balancer will balance to.

http.description: Configuration option for protocols http and https.
